title: ophrys bertolonii subsp. landaueri
intro: Ophrys bertolonii subsp. landaueri, also known as the Greenfly Orchid, is a subspecies of orchid native to Italy and parts of Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and aiding in wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
